title: Divisors
description: Your task in this problem is to determine the number of divisors of $n \choose k$. Just for fun -- or do you need any special reason for such a useful computation?
input: The input consists of several instances. Each instance consists of a single line containing two integers n and k (0 ≤ k ≤ n ≤ 431), separated by a single space.
output: For each instance, output a line containing exactly one integer -- the number of distinct divisors of $n \choose k$. For the input instances, this number does not exceed 263 - 1.
source: ICPC, Regionals, Europe, Central European Regional Contest, CTU Open Contest, CTU Open Contest 2005
